examples.title: Send Basic Email via SMTP
examples.description: Send a simple text email using SMTP
examples.code:
send_email(
    to="user@example.com",
    subject="Hello World",
    body="This is a test email from Email MCP Server"
)

examples.title: Send HTML Email
examples.description: Send an email with HTML formatting
examples.code:
send_email( to="user@example.com", subject="Welcome Message", body="Please view this email in HTML mode", html="

Welcome!

Thank you for joining our service.

  • Feature 1
  • Feature 2
" )

examples.title: Send with CC/BCC
examples.description: Send email with carbon copy recipients
examples.code:
send_email(
    to="primary@example.com",
    cc=["manager@example.com"],
    bcc=["admin@example.com"],
    subject="Team Update",
    body="Important information for the team"
)

examples.title: Use Specific Email Service
examples.description: Send email via SendGrid instead of default SMTP
examples.code:
send_email(
    to="user@example.com",
    subject="Transactional Email",
    body="Your order has been processed",
    service="sendgrid"
)

examples.title: Send Bulk Email Campaign
examples.description: Send marketing emails to multiple recipients
examples.code:
recipients = ["customer1@example.com", "customer2@example.com", "customer3@example.com"]
for email in recipients:
    send_email(
        to=email,
        subject="Special Offer Inside!",
        html=newsletter_template,
        service="sendgrid"
    )

examples.title: Send Email with Template
examples.code:
template_vars = {"name": "John", "company": "Acme Corp"}
send_email(
    to="john@customer.com",
    subject="Welcome to {{company}}",
    html=template.render(**template_vars),
    service="mailgun"
)

examples.title: Send Urgent Notification
examples.code:
send_email(
    to=["oncall@company.com", "manager@company.com"],
    subject="🚨 CRITICAL: System Down",
    body="Production system is experiencing outage. Immediate attention required.",

)

examples.title: Check Inbox for Unread Emails
examples.code:
result = check_inbox(limit=10, unread_only=True)
print(f"Found {result['count']} unread emails")
for email in result['emails']:
    print(f"From: {email['from']}, Subject: {email['subject']}")

examples.title: Check Specific Email Folder
examples.code:
result = check_inbox(folder="Sent", limit=5)
print(f"Found {result['count']} sent emails")

examples.title: Filter Emails by Date
examples.description: Get emails from the last week
examples.code:
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
week_ago = (datetime.now() - timedelta(days=7)).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
result = check_inbox(after_date=week_ago)
print(f"Emails from last week: {result['count']}")

# Send to multiple channels

examples.title: List Available Services
examples.description: Get list of configured email services
examples.code:
services = list_services()
print(f"Total services: {services['count']}")
for name, info in services['services'].items():
    print(f"{name}: {info['type']} - {'enabled' if info['enabled'] else 'disabled'}")

examples.title: Check Service Health
examples.description: Test connectivity to all email services
examples.code:
status = email_status()
print(f"Total services: {status['total_services']}")
print(f"Connected: {status['connected_services']}")
for name, info in status['services'].items():
    status_text = 'connected' if info['connected'] else 'disconnected'
    print(f"{name}: {status_text}")

examples.title: Newsletter Campaign
examples.code:
def send_newsletter_campaign(subscribers, newsletter):
    successful = 0
    failed = 0
    
    for subscriber in subscribers:
        try:
            personalized_content = newsletter.template.render(
                name=subscriber.name,
                preferences=subscriber.preferences,
                unsubscribe_url=generate_unsubscribe_url(subscriber.id)
            )
            
            send_email(
                to=subscriber.email,
                subject=newsletter.subject,
                html=personalized_content,
                service="mailgun"  # Better for marketing emails
            )
            successful += 1
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Failed to send to {subscriber.email}: {e}")
            failed += 1
    
    # Send campaign summary
    send_email(
        to="marketing@company.com",
        subject="Newsletter Campaign Complete",
        body=f"Sent to {successful} subscribers, {failed} failures",
        service="sendgrid"
    )

send_newsletter_campaign(active_subscribers, monthly_newsletter)

examples.title: Backup Notifications
examples.code:
def notify_backup_status(backup_result):
    if backup_result['success']:
        send_email(
            to="admin@company.com",
            subject="✅ Backup Completed Successfully",
            body=f"""
            Backup completed successfully!
            
            Size: {backup_result['size_mb']} MB
            Duration: {backup_result['duration_seconds']} seconds
            Location: {backup_result['location']}
            """,
            service="sendgrid"
        )
    else:
        send_email(
            to=["admin@company.com", "#alerts"],
            subject="🚨 BACKUP FAILED",
            body=f"""
            CRITICAL: Backup operation failed!
            
            Error: {backup_result['error']}
            Time: {backup_result['timestamp']}
            
            Immediate attention required.
            """,
            service="slack"
        )

# Usage after backup operation
notify_backup_status(backup_result)

examples.code:
def send_tracked_email(to, subject, body, campaign_id): # Add tracking pixel and links tracking_pixel = f'' tracked_body = body + tracking_pixel result = send_email( to=to, subject=subject, html=tracked_body, service="mailgun" # Better tracking support ) if result['success']: # Log for analytics log_email_sent(campaign_id, to, result.get('message_id')) return result send_tracked_email( to='customer@example.com', subject='Special Offer', body='

Check out our special offer!

', campaign_id='summer-sale-2024' )

examples.title: Attachment Handling
examples.code:
# Note: Attachments require additional implementation
# This is a conceptual example

def send_invoice_email(customer, invoice_pdf_path):
    # Read invoice file
    with open(invoice_pdf_path, 'rb') as f:
        invoice_data = f.read()
    
    send_email(
        to=customer.email,
        subject=f'Invoice #{customer.invoice_number}',
        body=f"""
        Dear {customer.name},
        
        Please find your invoice attached.
        
        Invoice #: {customer.invoice_number}
        Amount: ${customer.amount:.2f}
        Due Date: {customer.due_date}
        
        Thank you for your business!
        """,
        attachments=[{
            'filename': f'invoice_{customer.invoice_number}.pdf',
            'content': invoice_data,
            'content_type': 'application/pdf'
        }],
        service="sendgrid"  # Supports attachments
    )

send_invoice_email(customer, '/path/to/invoice.pdf')
